title: Podman rootless container with a custom network succeeds or fails based on which home directory the user has
body:
### Issue Description

I have encountered a bizarre issue running rootless containers within a user instance of systemd. When I attempt to create a network and run a container inside that network, podman succeeds or fails depending on the home directory of the user.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Start with a pristine stock Debian trixie VM.

Prepare the system:
```
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.ping_group_range="0 4294967293"
sudo apt install --no-install-recommends podman uidmap netavark aardvark-dns iptables slirp4netns
```

Create two identical users. The only difference is that `yc` lives under `/home` and `nc` lives under `/var/lib`:
```
sudo groupadd -r -g 201 yc
sudo useradd -r -s /usr/sbin/nologin -l -d /home/yc -M -g yc -u 201 yc
sudo mkdir -m 770 /home/yc
sudo chown -R yc:yc /home/yc
printf 'yc:231072:65536\n' | sudo tee -a /etc/subuid
printf 'yc:231072:65536\n' | sudo tee -a /etc/subgid
sudo loginctl enable-linger yc

sudo groupadd -r -g 202 nc
sudo useradd -r -s /usr/sbin/nologin -l -d /var/lib/nc -M -g nc -u 202 nc
sudo mkdir -m 770 /var/lib/nc
sudo chown -R nc:nc /var/lib/nc
printf 'nc:296608:65536\n' | sudo tee -a /etc/subuid
printf 'nc:296608:65536\n' | sudo tee -a /etc/subgid
sudo loginctl enable-linger nc
```

Switch to `yc`, create a network, start a container:
```
sudo su -Pl yc -s /bin/sh -c 'XDG_RUNTIME_DIR="/run/user/$(id -u)" DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/bus" export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS && bash'
$ podman network create mynetyc
mynetyc
$ podman network inspect mynetyc
[
     {
          "name": "mynetyc",
          "id": "88439a2ba7d8e2fb3a3e46f314c2cc1582bd5aabbbaeb3bec3ab1855bc1ac21e",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2024-03-31T22:59:32.217311329+03:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
$ podman run --rm -it --net=mynetyc alpine ping www.google.com
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob bca4290a9639 done   |
Copying config ace17d5d88 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
PING www.google.com (216.58.212.132): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 216.58.212.132: seq=0 ttl=42 time=14.068 ms
64 bytes from 216.58.212.132: seq=1 ttl=42 time=19.104 ms
^C
```

Exit, switch to `nc`, and try exactly the same:
```
sudo su -Pl nc -s /bin/sh -c 'XDG_RUNTIME_DIR="/run/user/$(id -u)" DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/bus" export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS && bash'
$ podman network create mynetnc
mynetnc
$ podman network inspect mynetnc
[
     {
          "name": "mynetnc",
          "id": "9266bfed7a2989835396f4be4295abd2bb8b11970e31f37a951629b08f1512f1",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2024-03-31T23:03:38.320782544+03:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
$ podman run --rm -it --net=mynetnc alpine ping www.google.com
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob bca4290a9639 done   |
Copying config ace17d5d88 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: unable to find network with name or ID mynetnc: network not found
```

Running the latter command again with debug logs:
```
$ podman run --log-level=debug --rm -it --net=mynetnc alpine ping www.google.com
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called run.PersistentPreRunE(podman run --log-level=debug --rm -it --net=mynetnc alpine ping www.google.com)
```

### Describe the results you received

Using the container via the `nc` user results in `Error: unable to find network with name or ID mynetnc: network not found`.

### Describe the results you expected

Running the container via the `nc` user should succeed just like with the `yc` user.

comments.body:
This seems to be a duplicate of #18162 
I have replicated your exact steps and have the same issue; creating _/var/lib/cni_ on the host gets rid the issue.
Correct that is a dup, and in the meantime I fixed that for podman 5.0 to no longer over mount /var/lib when netavark is in use so that bug is properly fixed now.
